skill.name: supabase-automation
skill.description: Automate Supabase administration through Rube MCP, including project discovery, schema inspection, table queries, SQL execution, storage checks, and edge function review.
skill.summary: Use Rube MCP to inspect and manage Supabase projects, schemas, SQL, storage, and service health.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static command-execution findings are false positives caused by Markdown backticks around MCP tool names and SQL examples, not executable Ruby or shell code. The skill has legitimate but elevated operational risk because it guides authenticated Supabase SQL execution, project administration, API key retrieval, and connection to the Rube MCP endpoint.

medium_findings.title: Arbitrary Supabase SQL Execution Capability
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs use of SUPABASE_BETA_RUN_SQL_QUERY for complex queries, inserts, updates, deletes, and SQL statements. This is an intended admin feature, but mistakes or misuse can modify or delete production data.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines explicitly document arbitrary SQL execution and write operations. The skill also includes safety guidance, so this is operational risk rather than confirmed malicious intent.

medium_findings.title: Sensitive Supabase API Key Access
medium_findings.description: The skill includes GET_PROJECT_API_KEYS, which returns live service-role keys. The file warns not to log, display, or persist full values, but the capability can expose highly sensitive credentials if mishandled.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The skill directly states that the tool returns live service-role keys. The surrounding warnings reduce intent concern but do not remove the credential exposure risk.

medium_findings.title: Third-Party MCP Connection for Supabase Administration
medium_findings.description: The setup directs users to add the Rube MCP endpoint and authenticate Supabase through that connection. This is expected for the skill, but it creates a trust dependency for privileged database operations.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The instructions clearly require Rube MCP and a Supabase connection. This appears legitimate, but administrators should understand the third-party access path before use.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backtick Command-Execution False Positives
low_findings.description: The numerous Ruby or shell backtick findings are caused by Markdown formatting for MCP tool names, SQL syntax, parameters, and examples. No executable Ruby, shell script, or command interpolation was found in SKILL.md.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file is Markdown documentation and the backticks wrap literal tool names and parameter names. No code block or instruction executes shell commands locally.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm False Positive
low_findings.description: The scanner flagged weak cryptography at lines 3 and 46, but these lines describe the skill and a sort expression parameter. No hashing, encryption, or cryptographic algorithm usage was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain descriptive text and an order example. They do not include MD5, SHA1, DES, or other cryptographic operations.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance False Positive
low_findings.description: The scanner flagged reconnaissance at lines 61 and 185, but these lines describe a SQL query parameter and timeout mitigation. They do not collect host, process, network, or operating system information.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The semantic context is database query guidance, not local system inspection. No evidence found for host reconnaissance behavior.

dangerous_patterns.title: Privileged SQL Mutation Workflow
dangerous_patterns.description: The documented workflow can perform INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE, DDL, and complex SQL through Supabase. Users should require explicit confirmation before writes and prefer read_only for SELECT statements.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The capability is explicitly documented across the database workflow and SQL best-practice sections. It is powerful but aligned with the skill purpose.

dangerous_patterns.title: Live Secret Retrieval Workflow
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill documents a tool that returns project API keys, including service-role keys. Secret values must be masked and should not be persisted in transcripts or files.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The file directly identifies returned API keys as live secrets. The warning text confirms the sensitivity of this workflow.

content.value_statement: Supabase teams often need safe ways to inspect projects, schemas, tables, and service health from an AI assistant. This skill provides structured Rube MCP workflows for database administration, SQL execution, storage review, and edge function inspection.
actual_capabilities: Lists Supabase organizations and projects through Rube MCP tools.
Inspects tables, views, columns, indexes, relationships, and schema metadata.
Queries table rows with filters, ordering, limits, and pagination guidance.
Runs SQL for complex reads and controlled write operations.
Checks project configuration, API keys, service health, storage buckets, and edge functions.
Generates TypeScript types from selected Supabase schemas.
limitations: Requires Rube MCP and an active authenticated Supabase connection.
Database writes and API key retrieval depend on the user account permissions.
Edge function support is limited to listing and retrieving metadata.
Complex DDL or large listings can time out and may need smaller batches.

use_cases.title: Inspect production schema safely
use_cases.description: Review tables, columns, constraints, indexes, and relationships before changing application code.

use_cases.title: Audit Supabase project configuration
use_cases.description: List projects, organizations, members, auth settings, service health, and storage buckets from one workflow.

use_cases.title: Support controlled data operations
use_cases.description: Run read-only checks first, then prepare explicit SQL changes with schema context and confirmation.

prompt_templates.title: List my Supabase projects
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the Supabase automation skill to search current Rube tools, confirm my connection, and list my Supabase projects with references.

prompt_templates.title: Inspect a table schema
prompt_templates.prompt: Find my project reference, list public tables, then inspect the schema for the orders and customers tables. Summarize columns, keys, indexes, and relationships.

prompt_templates.title: Run a safe read-only query
prompt_templates.prompt: Query recent failed payments in my Supabase database. Use schema discovery first, set read_only for SQL, and return only aggregated results.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a controlled migration
prompt_templates.prompt: Inspect the target schema, identify dependencies, draft a PostgreSQL migration plan, and ask for confirmation before any write query is executed.

output_examples.input: Show me what tables exist in my Supabase project.
output_examples.output: A concise table inventory with schema names, table or view type, row count estimates when available, and notes about missing metadata.

output_examples.input: Check whether my Supabase services are healthy.
output_examples.output: A service health summary for auth, database, pooler, realtime, REST, and storage, with permission errors separated from actual outages.

output_examples.input: Prepare a SQL update for a customer record.
output_examples.output: A staged plan that verifies the table schema, previews matching rows, explains the proposed update, and requests confirmation before execution.

best_practices: Search current Rube MCP tool schemas before every workflow.
Use read_only for SELECT queries and require confirmation before writes.
Mask API keys and avoid storing full secrets in files or transcripts.
anti_patterns: Running SQL writes before inspecting table schemas and target rows.
Displaying full service-role keys or project secrets in assistant output.
Assuming organization IDs, slugs, and project references are interchangeable.

faq.question: Does this skill require Supabase API keys in the prompt?
faq.answer: No. It uses an authenticated Rube MCP Supabase connection and should not receive raw secrets in prompts.

faq.question: Can it modify database data?
faq.answer: Yes. It documents SQL execution for writes, so users should require explicit confirmation before mutations.

faq.question: Can it deploy Supabase Edge Functions?
faq.answer: No. The documented edge function tools list and retrieve function metadata only.

faq.question: How should API keys be handled?
faq.answer: API keys should be masked or truncated. Full values should not be logged, displayed, or persisted.

faq.question: Why should tools be searched first?
faq.answer: Rube MCP tool schemas can change. Searching first helps the assistant use current parameters.

faq.question: Is this suitable for production projects?
faq.answer: Yes, with admin controls. Use read-only checks first, review planned writes, and restrict permissions.
